summary: Iran war trajectory and the Fed's hawkish pivot are the twin forces pressing on markets. Trump's signal of continued strikes kept oil near $110-$112 a barrel this week while choking off Fed easing hopes; the FOMC cut its 2026 rate-cut projections from three to just one. The March jobs report landed today with markets dark, leaving the data unpriced until Monday.
tweet: Iran war keeps oil above $110 and the Fed on hold — the March jobs report hit this morning with every exchange closed, leaving the week's final catalyst unpriced until Monday.
